draft.title: weather app for runners
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
weather app for runners

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: A clean, high-contrast design with lots of white space will feel energetic and focused—helping runners quickly scan weather info mid-workout.
facade.label: Bright minimal interface
facade.acceptImplies: User leans toward sharp, tech-forward, low-friction UX. Likely prefers glanceable data over atmospheric immersion.
facade.rejectImplies: User may want more warmth, texture, or contextual richness—perhaps design that feels grounded or moody rather than stark.

evidence.hypothesis: A clean, high-contrast design with lots of white space will feel energetic and focused—helping runners quickly scan weather info mid-workout.
evidence.implication: User leans toward sharp, tech-forward, low-friction UX. Likely prefers glanceable data over atmospheric immersion.

facade.hypothesis: A voice that actively celebrates the runner's effort and milestones in real-time, using enthusiastic language and positive reinforcement rather than neutral reporting.
facade.label: Cheering you on
facade.acceptImplies: The app should use conversational, motivational copy; celebratory notifications; maybe a virtual coach persona that feels like a supportive training partner.
facade.rejectImplies: The app should maintain a neutral, objective tone focused on data delivery; notifications should be factual and understated; no personality or encouragement layer.

facade.hypothesis: Runners prioritize real-time stats (pace, HR, distance) over forecast narrative—displayed as a compact, always-visible ribbon rather than scrollable panels or text-heavy cards.
facade.label: Live metrics strip
facade.acceptImplies: Surface vital numbers instantly; minimize secondary info; assume users glance mid-run.
facade.rejectImplies: Runners want contextual story (e.g., 'wind picking up, slow down') or scattered single-metric displays rather than unified data bands.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer high-contrast, energetic UI that feels performance-focused rather than soft and approachable
facade.axisTargeted: light vs dark / vibrant vs muted
facade.label: Neon accents, dark background
facade.acceptImplies: The app should use dark mode with bright accent colors (neon green, electric blue) to signal speed and intensity—matching the runner's mental state
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer softer, more calming visual language (pastels, light backgrounds, warm tones) that doesn't amp up stress during runs

facade.hypothesis: Runners need real-time feedback on their current speed; a subtle rhythmic visual (pulsing dot or ring) synced to cadence creates tactile-like feedback without requiring a glance away from route
facade.axisTargeted: Motion and behavior — animated feedback vs static readout
facade.label: Pace indicator pulse
facade.acceptImplies: User values continuous ambient awareness of pace; favors breathing-synchronized or beat-locked animations; willing to accept CPU cost for immersive feel
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers glanceable numeric displays; finds animation distracting during effort; prioritizes battery life and instant clarity over ambient motion cues

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
Whether user wants quick-glance info at top vs. scrollable detailed forecast layers
Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
Whether user wants calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity) vs. standard weather only
Whether user prefers smooth, gradual transitions vs. snappy, instant updates and alerts

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants quick-glance info at top vs. scrollable detailed forecast layers
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity) vs. standard weather only
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers smooth, gradual transitions vs. snappy, instant updates and alerts
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Runners prefer persistent sidebar nav (always visible route/weather) paired with scrollable cards for current conditions—combines stability with glanceability during runs
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation persistence vs. full-screen focus; information architecture (sidebar + cards vs. tabs + lists)
facade.label: Sidebar with live cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick info lookup without losing context; sidebar-heavy layouts will likely test well; persistent nav beats tab-switching for active use cases
facade.rejectImplies: User prioritizes immersive, distraction-free view; full-screen, modal-driven, or tab-based layouts may resonate better; sidebar feels cluttered or unnecessary in running context

facade.hypothesis: Hand-drawn or retro-style weather symbols will feel organic and approachable—creating a warmer, less clinical mood that might appeal to runners who value comfort alongside performance data.
facade.label: Analog weather icons
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward human, tactile, slightly nostalgic design language. May prefer personality over pure minimalism.
facade.rejectImplies: User wants sleek, contemporary, tech-forward aesthetics. Confirm preference for sharp/digital mood over warm/analog.

draft.summary: A weather app designed for runners, showing real-time conditions, wind impact, and run-friendly forecasts at a glance.
